rationale: The page contains detailed technical information and objectives for two major space missions: the NISAR (NASA-ISRO) satellite and the Gaganyaan mission. It includes specific details about radar bands, launch vehicles, orbits, and mission objectives, which are highly relevant for competitive exams.
cleanedText:
4. SPACE TECHNOLOGY
4.1. NISAR (NASA-ISRO SYNTHETIC APERTURE RADAR) SATELLITE
Why in the News?
After the successful launch of NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR) satellite in July 2025, the 12m diameter Antenna reflector has been successfully deployed.
About NISAR Mission
• NISAR is a global, microwave imaging mission, with capability to acquire fully polarimetric and interferometric data.
o It is world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band and S-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) on a single platform.
Objectives of NISAR Mission
• Measure the woody biomass and its changes.
• Track changes in the extent of active crops.
• Understand the changes in wetlands' extent.
• Map Greenland's & Antarctica's ice sheets, dynamics of sea ice and mountain glaciers.
• Characterize land surface deformation related to seismicity, volcanism, landslides, and subsidence & uplift associated with changes in subsurface aquifers, hydrocarbon reservoirs, etc.
• NASA provided the L-band radar, GPS receiver, high-rate telecom system, etc.
• ISRO contributed the S-band radar, the spacecraft bus, etc.
• Launch Vehicle: ISRO’s Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV)-F16.
o It was launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• Orbit: Sun Synchronous Polar Orbit
o It is the first time a GSLV rocket was used to place a satellite into a Sun-Synchronous orbit (SSO).
o GSLV is typically used for placement in Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit/GTO.
• Mission Life: 5 years
Technical Features
• SweepSAR (Swept Synthetic Aperture Radar): Produces high-resolution, wide-swath images across a broad range of terrain.
o In SAR, an instrument sends out a pulse of energy and then records the amount of that energy reflected back after it interacts with Earth.
• Repeat Cycle: Provides high-resolution data every 12 days to monitor land changes across Earth.
• Antenna and Resolution: The satellite features a large, 12-metre diameter common unfurlable reflector antenna.
• Open-Data Policy: Information collected will be freely available to global scientific community.
4.2. GAGANYAAN MISSION
Why in the News?
ISRO successfully completes first Integrated Air Drop Test for Gaganyaan Mission.
More on the News
• Its objective was to validate the parachute system that will bring back astronauts safely under the Gaganyaan mission.
